purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Post-merge run 29675302707, failed Windows Node 18 job 88161565040, successful unchanged rerun 88162363857, live required-check audit, independent fail-open review, and the immutable release evidence as delta base.
outputs: A non-flaky, fail-closed CI performance policy with immutable action pins, executable anti-bypass tests, separate hotfix lineage, and delta-linked implementation evidence.
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
All Node.js tests pass locally on Node 24.
Package scripts keep an explicit enforcing enterprise benchmark command and a combined local enterprise verification command.
CI runs correctness on all nine OS/runtime cells and runs the benchmark exactly once as a separate fail-closed step inside the required Ubuntu Node 24 context.
CI workflow guards reject mutable action pins, continue-on-error and job-level skip bypasses.
Tagged-release verification runs correctness on all nine cells and the enforcing benchmark only on Ubuntu Node 24.
Strict story gate passes without invalidating completed release lineage.

contextualization.summary: Stabilize the post-release CI without changing support or SLOs: keep correctness on all nine OS/runtime cells and enforce the unchanged enterprise benchmark exactly once inside the existing required Ubuntu Node 24 check. Record the hotfix evidence as a delta from, never a replacement for, the completed release evidence.
constraints: Do not raise or weaken existing benchmark thresholds.
Do not remove Node 18.18, Node 20, Node 24, Ubuntu, macOS or Windows correctness coverage.
Do not change repository protection settings; bind the benchmark to the already required Ubuntu Node 24 context.
Do not rewrite or replace the completed ST-ENT-RELEASE-R1 story, task-start receipt or output; use its implementation evidence only as the immutable delta base.
No npm publication, tag, GitHub release, production access, secrets or history rewrite.

approvals.summary: Delegated one-use approval of the dedicated hotfix delta contract: frozen fail-closed CI scope, unchanged thresholds/support, and completed release evidence preserved as the immutable base.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Approve only the dedicated hotfix delta contract and implementation-evidence format, preserving completed release evidence as its immutable base while applying the frozen fail-closed CI scope; no threshold, support, repository-setting, deployment, release, secret, destructive, or unrelated change.
